mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production signal immediate supply chain bottlenecks and reduced economic activity, causing rapid negative repricing in equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during periods of INDPRO contraction exceeding 1.0% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with stagnant federal funds rates erodes real yields and prompts market expectations of aggressive future tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3% while FEDFUNDS change < 0.05%
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS stability shows a positive or zero Rank IC over 20-day horizons in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumption capacity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns exhibit a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP exceeding 0.5% indicates an economic contraction that reduces earnings forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below the -0.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and payroll growth reveals productivity shocks that disrupt earnings stability, causing immediate negative equity market reactions over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0% while PAYEMS month-over-month change > 0.2%
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant negative predictive power when the specified INDPRO-PAYEMS divergence occurs, with Rank IC > -0.01.
